recipes.name: Dolce Verde Cranberry Biscotti
ingredients: 1/2 cup shelled pistachio nuts, roughly chopped
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a large bowl, whisk together flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt.
In a separate bowl, lightly beat eggs with vanilla and almond extracts. Add wet ingredients to dry and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Fold in chopped pistachios and dried cranberries until evenly distributed. Turn dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead briefly until it comes together.
Divide dough in half. Shape each half into a log, approximately 10-12 inches long and 1.5-2 inches wide. Place logs on the prepared baking sheet.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until golden brown and firm. Remove from oven and reduce temperature to 300°F (150°C).
Let logs cool for 10-15 minutes, then carefully transfer to a cutting board. Using a serrated knife, slice each log into 1/2-inch thick pieces. Lay slices cut-side down on the baking sheet.
Return to oven and bake for another 15-20 minutes, flipping halfway, until dry and crisp. Let cool completely. Melt white almond bark according to package directions and drizzle over cooled biscotti.

recipes.name: Empanadas Dulces de Pistacho y Arándano con Glaseado de Almendra
ingredients: 1/2 cup cold water (plus more if needed)
1/2 cup shelled pistachio nuts, finely chopped (for filling)
1/2 cup dried cranberries, finely chopped (for filling)
steps: **Prepare the Dough:** In a large bowl, whisk together flour, 1/4 cup sugar, baking powder, and 1/2 tsp salt. Add 1/4 cup olive oil and mix until crumbly. Gradually add cold water, mixing until a dough forms. Knead on a lightly floured surface for 5-7 minutes until smooth and elastic. Cover and let rest for 30 minutes.

**Prepare the Filling:** In a small saucepan, combine chopped pistachios, cranberries, 1/4 cup sugar, 1/2 tsp almond extract, 1/4 tsp vanilla extract, and 2 tbsp water. Cook over medium heat for 5-7 minutes, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens slightly and most of the liquid is absorbed. Remove from heat and let cool completely.
Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Divide the rested dough into 12 equal pieces. Roll each piece into a 4-5 inch circle. Place a tablespoon of filling on one half of each circle.
Fold the dough over the filling to create a half-moon shape. Press the edges firmly to seal, then crimp with a fork. Place empanadas on the prepared baking sheet.
Beat the remaining egg with a tablespoon of water to make an egg wash. Brush the tops of the empanadas with the egg wash.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown. Let cool slightly on a wire rack.
**Prepare the Glaze:** Melt white almond bark according to package directions. Stir in 1 tsp almond extract. Drizzle the glaze over the cooled empanadas before serving.

recipes.name: Gulab Pista Cranberry Cake Bites
ingredients: 1/2 cup shelled pistachio nuts, finely ground (plus extra for garnish)
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Lightly grease and flour a mini muffin tin or a 8x8 inch baking pan.
In a large bowl, whisk together flour, baking powder, ground cardamom, and salt.
In a separate bowl, whisk together sugar, eggs, olive oil, milk, vanilla extract, and almond extract until well combined.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and mix with a spatula until just combined. Do not overmix.
Gently fold in the finely ground pistachios and chopped dried cranberries.
Spoon the batter into the prepared mini muffin tin (filling about 2/3 full) or pour into the 8x8 inch pan.
Bake for 20-25 minutes for cake bites (or 30-35 minutes for an 8x8 pan), or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean. Let cool completely.
Melt white almond bark according to package directions. Drizzle over the cooled cake bites and sprinkle with a few extra finely ground pistachios for garnish.

recipes.name: Hua Sheng Tao (Pistachio Blossom) Fried Doughnuts
ingredients: 1/4 cup shelled pistachio nuts, finely ground (for dough and garnish)
1/4 cup dried cranberries, finely chopped (for dough)
steps: In a large bowl, whisk together flour, sugar, baking powder, salt, finely ground pistachios, and chopped dried cranberries.
In a separate bowl, whisk together egg, milk, 1 tbsp olive oil, vanilla extract, and almond extract.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and mix until a sticky dough forms. Knead lightly on a well-floured surface for 2-3 minutes until smooth enough to handle.
Roll out the dough to about 1/4-inch thickness. Use a small cookie cutter (e.g., 2-inch round) to cut out doughnut shapes. You can also cut into small squares or diamond shapes.
Heat 3-4 cups of olive oil in a deep pot or Dutch oven over medium heat to 350-375°F (175-190°C).

Carefully drop 3-4 doughnuts into the hot oil (do not overcrowd). Fry for 2-3 minutes per side, or until golden brown and puffed. Remove with a slotted spoon and place on a wire rack lined with paper towels to drain excess oil.
Let the doughnuts cool slightly. Melt white almond bark according to package directions. You can add a drop of almond extract to the melted bark if desired.
Dip or drizzle cooled doughnuts with the melted almond bark, and sprinkle with a pinch of finely ground pistachios before the bark sets.

recipes.name: Zahra Pista Zayt (Orange Pistachio Olive Oil Cake)
ingredients: 1/2 cup shelled pistachio nuts, roughly chopped
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease and flour a 9-inch round cake pan or a loaf pan.
In a large bowl, whisk together flour, baking powder, and salt.
In a separate large bowl, whisk sugar and eggs until light and fluffy (about 2-3 minutes).
Gradually whisk in the olive oil, milk, vanilla extract, almond extract, orange zest, and orange juice (and orange blossom water, if using) until well combined.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and mix with a spatula until just combined. Do not overmix.
Gently fold in the chopped pistachios and dried cranberries.
Pour the batter into the prepared cake pan. Bake for 40-45 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.

Let the cake cool in the pan for 10 minutes before inverting onto a wire rack to cool completely. Once cool, melt white almond bark according to package directions and drizzle generously over the cake.

recipes.name: Tuscan Sunrise Biscotti with White Chocolate Drizzle
ingredients: ½ cup shelled pistachio nuts, roughly chopped
1 tbsp olive oil (plus more for greasing)
4 oz white almond bark, for drizzling
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Lightly grease a baking sheet with olive oil.
In a large bowl, whisk together flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt.
In a separate bowl, whisk eggs, vanilla extract, almond extract, and 1 tbsp olive oil until well combined.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients. Mix until a shaggy dough forms, then fold in chopped pistachios and dried cranberries until evenly distributed.
Divide the dough in half. On the prepared baking sheet, form each half into a log, approximately 10-12 inches long and 2 inches wide.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown and firm. Remove from oven and reduce temperature to 300°F (150°C).
While still warm, carefully transfer logs to a cutting board. Using a serrated knife, slice each log diagonally into ½-inch thick cookies. Place cookies cut-side down back onto the baking sheet.
Bake for another 15-20 minutes, flipping halfway through, until dry and crisp. Let cool completely. Melt white almond bark according to package directions and drizzle over the cooled biscotti.

recipes.name: Fiesta Pistachio Buñuelos with Almond Bark Glaze
ingredients: 2 tbsp olive oil (plus 3-4 cups for deep frying)
steps: In a large bowl, combine flour, 2 tbsp granulated sugar, baking powder, and salt. In a separate bowl, whisk eggs, vanilla extract, almond extract, and 2 tbsp olive oil.
Gradually add the wet ingredients to the dry, mixing until a soft, pliable dough forms. If too sticky, add a little more flour; if too dry, add a tiny bit of water. Knead for 2-3 minutes.
Divide the dough into 12-15 equal pieces. On a lightly floured surface, roll each piece into a very thin circle (about 4-5 inches in diameter).
Heat 3-4 cups of olive oil in a deep pan or Dutch oven to 350°F (175°C). Fry buñuelos one or two at a time for 1-2 minutes per side, until golden brown and crispy. Drain on paper towels.

While buñuelos cool, melt white almond bark according to package directions. Stir in 1/4 cup powdered sugar and 1-2 teaspoons of water to create a thick glaze consistency. Add more water if needed, a few drops at a time.
Dip each buñuelo halfway into the almond bark glaze, or drizzle generously over them. Immediately sprinkle with finely chopped pistachios and dried cranberries before the glaze sets.
Serve warm or at room temperature.

recipes.name: Regal Saffron & Pistachio Halwa
ingredients: ½ cup shelled pistachio nuts, chopped (plus more for garnish)
¼ cup dried cranberries, chopped (plus more for garnish)
¼ tsp ground cardamom (optional, common pantry staple)
Few strands of saffron (optional, for color and flavor)
steps: In a small bowl, dissolve saffron strands in 2 tablespoons of the hot water and set aside.

Heat olive oil in a heavy-bottomed pan or skillet over medium-low heat. Add flour and stir continuously, roasting until the flour turns light golden brown and smells fragrant (about 8-10 minutes). Be careful not to burn it.
In another pan, combine the remaining hot water, sugar, salt, vanilla extract, almond extract, and cardamom powder (if using). Heat until sugar dissolves, then add the saffron water.
Gradually pour the hot sugar mixture into the roasted flour mixture, stirring constantly to prevent lumps. The mixture will thicken quickly.
Continue stirring on low heat until the halwa pulls away from the sides of the pan and has a smooth, pudding-like consistency (about 5-7 minutes).
Stir in the chopped pistachio nuts and dried cranberries.
Transfer the halwa to a serving dish. Garnish with additional chopped pistachios and dried cranberries.
Serve warm or at room temperature. Can be molded into desired shapes if serving for a special occasion.

recipes.name: Jade Garden Steamed Almond Pistachio Cake
ingredients: ½ cup finely ground pistachio nuts
¼ cup finely chopped dried cranberries
steps: Lightly grease an 8-inch round cake pan or heat-proof bowl. Prepare a steamer by bringing water to a boil.
In a large bowl, whisk together flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt.
In a separate bowl, whisk eggs until light and frothy. Add olive oil, vanilla extract, almond extract, and warm water; mix well.
Gradually add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients, mixing until just combined and smooth. Do not overmix.
Gently fold in the finely ground pistachio nuts and chopped dried cranberries.
Pour the batter into the prepared cake pan. Cover the pan tightly with foil or a lid to prevent water from dripping into the cake.
Place the cake pan in the steamer. Steam over medium-high heat for 30-35 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
Carefully remove from the steamer, uncover, and let cool for 10 minutes before inverting onto a wire rack. Serve warm or at room temperature.

recipes.name: Emerald Isle Fritters with Siam Dip
ingredients: ½ cup shelled pistachio nuts, roughly chopped
1 can (13.5 oz) full-fat coconut milk (common pantry staple)
steps: In a large bowl, whisk together flour, ½ cup sugar, baking powder, and salt.

In a separate bowl, whisk the egg, milk, vanilla extract, and almond extract until well combined. Gradually add the wet ingredients to the dry, mixing until just combined. Do not overmix; a few lumps are fine.
Gently fold in the chopped pistachio nuts and dried cranberries into the batter.
Heat olive oil in a large deep skillet or Dutch oven over medium-high heat to 350°F (175°C).
Carefully drop spoonfuls of batter (about 1-2 tablespoons each) into the hot oil. Fry fritters for 2-3 minutes per side, or until golden brown and cooked through. Do not overcrowd the pan.
Remove fritters with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels. Keep warm while you prepare the dip.

For the Siam Dip: In a small saucepan, combine coconut milk, ¼ cup sugar, almond extract, and a pinch of salt. Bring to a gentle simmer over medium heat, stirring until sugar dissolves. Cook for 5 minutes, allowing it to slightly thicken.
Serve the warm fritters immediately with the warm Siam Dip on the side.

recipes.name: Tuscan Sunset Pistachio & Cranberry Cantucci
ingredients: 0.5 cup shelled pistachio nuts, roughly chopped
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a large bowl, whisk together flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt.

In a separate bowl, whisk eggs, vanilla extract, almond extract, and olive oil until well combined. Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and mix until a shaggy dough forms. Fold in the chopped pistachios and dried cranberries.

Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and divide it in half. Form each half into a log, approximately 10-12 inches long and 2 inches wide. Place the logs on the prepared baking sheet, spaced apart.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until golden brown and firm to the touch. Remove from oven and let cool on the baking sheet for 10 minutes.

Reduce oven temperature to 300°F (150°C). Transfer the logs to a cutting board. Using a serrated knife, slice the logs diagonally into 0.5-inch thick pieces. Lay the slices cut-side down on the baking sheet.
Bake for another 15-20 minutes, flipping halfway through, until the cantucci are dry and crisp. Let cool completely on a wire rack.
Melt the white almond bark according to package directions. Drizzle the melted bark over the cooled cantucci or dip one end of each cantucci. Let set on parchment paper.

recipes.name: Mumbai Serenity Pistachio & Cardamom Tea Cake
ingredients: 0.5 cup shelled pistachio nuts, roughly chopped
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease and flour a 9x5 inch loaf pan or an 8x8 inch square pan.
In a large bowl, whisk together flour, baking powder, salt, and ground cardamom.
In a separate bowl, whisk together granulated sugar, eggs, olive oil, milk, vanilla extract, and almond extract until well combined.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and mix until just combined. Do not overmix. Gently fold in the chopped pistachio nuts and dried cranberries.
Pour the batter into the prepared pan and spread evenly. Bake for 30-35 minutes, or until a wooden skewer inserted into the center comes out clean.
Remove the cake from the oven and let it cool in the pan for 10 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack to cool completely.
Melt the white almond bark according to package directions. Drizzle the melted bark generously over the cooled tea cake. Allow the glaze to set before serving.

recipes.name: Fiesta Pistachio Cranberry Churro Bites
ingredients: 0.25 cup shelled pistachio nuts, finely chopped (for garnish)
0.25 cup dried cranberries, finely chopped (for garnish)
steps: In a medium saucepan, combine water, 2 tablespoons sugar, 0.25 teaspoon salt, and 2 tablespoons olive oil. Bring to a rolling boil over medium-high heat.
Remove from heat and immediately add the flour all at once. Stir vigorously with a wooden spoon until a ball of dough forms and pulls away from the sides of the pan. Let cool for 5 minutes.
Transfer the dough to a mixing bowl. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition until fully incorporated. Stir in the baking powder and vanilla extract.
Heat 4 cups of olive oil in a large deep pot or Dutch oven to 375°F (190°C). Place the dough in a piping bag fitted with a large star tip.

Carefully pipe 1-inch pieces of dough directly into the hot oil, using kitchen shears or a knife to cut the dough. Fry in batches for 2-3 minutes, turning occasionally, until golden brown and cooked through.
While the churros are hot, toss them in a mixture of 0.5 cup granulated sugar and ground cinnamon until evenly coated. Place on a platter.

Melt the white almond bark according to package directions. Stir in 0.5 teaspoon almond extract to create a smooth dipping sauce. Serve the churro bites immediately with the warm dipping sauce, garnished with chopped pistachios and dried cranberries.

recipes.name: Jade Garden Steamed Pistachio & Cranberry Fortune Cakes
ingredients: 0.5 cup milk (dairy or non-dairy) or water
0.5 cup shelled pistachio nuts, roughly chopped
steps: Prepare a steamer: fill a large pot with about 2 inches of water, bring to a boil. Line small heat-proof ramekins or silicone muffin molds with paper liners or grease them well. (Alternatively, use an 8-inch round cake pan).
In a large bowl, whisk eggs and granulated sugar until light and fluffy, about 3-4 minutes.
Add olive oil, milk, vanilla extract, and almond extract to the egg mixture, mixing until well combined.
In a separate bowl, whisk together flour, baking powder, and salt. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients, mixing until just combined. Do not overmix.
Gently fold in the chopped pistachio nuts and dried cranberries.
Divide the batter evenly among the prepared ramekins/molds, filling them about 3/4 full. Place the molds into the steamer basket, ensuring they don't touch each other.
Steam for 20-25 minutes (or 30-35 minutes for a larger cake), or until a wooden skewer inserted into the center comes out clean. Carefully remove from the steamer and let cool.
Melt the white almond bark according to package directions. Drizzle the melted bark over the cooled steamed cakes. Let set before serving.

recipes.name: Siam Serene Pistachio & Coconut Loaf
ingredients: 0.5 cup shelled pistachio nuts, roughly chopped
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease and flour a 9x5 inch loaf pan.
In a large bowl, whisk together flour, baking powder, salt, and desiccated coconut.
In a separate bowl, whisk together granulated sugar, eggs, olive oil, milk, vanilla extract, and almond extract until well combined.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and mix until just combined. Do not overmix. Gently fold in the chopped pistachio nuts and dried cranberries.
Pour the batter into the prepared loaf pan and spread evenly. Bake for 45-50 minutes, or until a wooden skewer inserted into the center comes out clean.
Remove the loaf from the oven and let it cool in the pan for 10 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack to cool completely.
Melt the white almond bark according to package directions. Drizzle the melted bark generously over the cooled loaf. Allow the glaze to set before slicing and serving.
